title: Python generated CLI completion promotion

execplan_profile.projection_rule: canonical_core is authoritative for intent, scope, next action, proof, continuation, and closeout; legacy fields remain compatibility projections.

canonical_core.requested_outcome: Promote the Python side of GitHub #892 only after generated parser, dispatch, interface, adapter metadata, proof routing, Docker conformance, and thin runtime-handler boundaries are all contract-backed and proven.
canonical_core.hard_constraints: Keep scope bounded to Python generated CLI completion. Do not expand into TypeScript, Bash, PowerShell, or mutation-capable generated adapters.
canonical_core.agent_may_decide: Bounded decomposition, touched-path narrowing, validation tightening, and plan-local residue routing.
canonical_core.escalate_when: A better-looking fix changes the requested outcome, owned surface, time horizon, or meaningful validation story.
canonical_core.next_action: Implement the schema-backed Python completion gate and prove it with generated package static, Python, Docker, planning summary, and closeout-trust checks.
proof_expectations: uv run agentic-workspace report --target . --section closeout_trust --format json
completion_criteria: command_package_ir.json records Python CLI completion as full only through a schema-backed completion gate rather than by reusing weak-agent-safe adapter maturity.
Static proof fails if full Python completion is claimed without the prerequisite parser, dispatch, interface, proof-gate, Docker conformance, and thin runtime-handler evidence.
Generated Python black-box conformance and Docker conformance pass against the generated package surfaces.
The active execplan and decomposition both record that this is Python-only completion, not full codegen-epic completion.

non_goals: Leave adjacent backlog or follow-on work out of this plan.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: Complete the Python side of #892 while preserving the broader codegen epic as separate from Bash, PowerShell, TypeScript, and future mutation-capable transports.
intent_continuity.continuation surface: GitHub #892 remains the broader codegen epic; this plan should not close non-Python lanes.

iterative_follow_through.next likely slice: continue the current milestone until the completion criteria are met

intent_interpretation.literal request: Keep working on the epic, scoped to Python first, and finish Python generated CLI completion without claiming unrelated lanes.
intent_interpretation.inferred intended outcome: Make Python CLI completion an implementation-independent, proof-gated state rather than a weak-agent adapter maturity label.
intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: Add a schema-backed Python completion gate to command_package_ir.json and make the generated package proof enforce it.
intent_interpretation.review guidance: Confirm the scaffolded plan still matches the promoted item before broad implementation.

execution_bounds.allowed paths: command package IR/schema, generated package proof runner tests, Python completion decomposition, and this execplan.
execution_bounds.max changed files: About 7 files, excluding regenerated output if the IR change requires it.
execution_bounds.required validation commands: uv run python scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py; uv run python scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py --python-conformance; uv run python scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py --python-docker-conformance --require-docker; uv run pytest tests/test_generated_command_package_proof_runner.py -q; uv run agentic-planning summary --target . --verbose --format json; uv run agentic-workspace report --target . --section closeout_trust --format json
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: Ask before broadening beyond the promoted item.
execution_bounds.stop before touching: Unrelated backlog, adjacent modules, or canonical contracts not named by this plan.

stop_conditions.stop when: The work no longer matches the promoted item or its completion criteria.
stop_conditions.escalate when boundary reached: A correct fix requires changing the requested outcome or ownership boundary.
stop_conditions.escalate on scope drift: Implementation needs files outside the filled execution bounds.
stop_conditions.escalate on proof failure: The selected proof cannot demonstrate the completion criteria.

context_budget.recoverable later: Repo background, historical reviews, and deferred backlog unless compact outputs point there.

context_budget.pre-work config pull: Use compact config/startup/summary outputs before opening raw planning or routing files.
context_budget.pre-work memory pull: Route to the narrowest relevant memory only when the task needs durable repo knowledge.
context_budget.tiny resumability note: Implement Python completion state and static gate, then prove it with generated package static/Python/Docker checks and closeout trust.
context_budget.context-shift triggers: Proof failure, scope drift, interruption, handoff, or closeout.

closure_check.accepted values: Accepted values: slice status = complete|completed|bounded slice complete; larger-intent status = closed|complete|completed for archive-and-close or open|partial|unfinished for archive-but-keep-lane-open; closure decision = archive-and-close|archive-but-keep-lane-open.
